---

# UC-13.1.11 · Indexer Cluster Bucket Replication Health (CMReplicationManager fixup tasks, primary-search-factor compliance, generation drift, peer rejoin storms, replication-queue saturation)

> **Criticality:** Critical &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** Platform &middot; **Type:** Reliability, Availability &middot; **Wave:** Walk &middot; **Status:** Verified

*We watch the copying and safety nets that keep search data consistent across machines. When that work piles up, peers flap, or copies fall out of policy, we raise a clear signal so teams fix the foundation before people notice missing or slow results.*

---

## Description

Detects indexer cluster replication and search-factor stress by fusing cluster manager CMReplicationManager fixup pressure, CMRepJob peer churn, CMMaster generation and compliance hints, high-signal _audit peer and cluster operations, and live manager REST peer status snapshots, then joining fleet metadata for routing.

## Value

Platform leaders shorten mean time to innocence when search looks flaky but the true failure is bucket copies lagging or primaries drifting, while finance and risk stakeholders receive an evidence trail that ties replication backlog to operator windows rather than silent data gaps. Capacity and incident teams gain a single saved search that differentiates healthy catch-up from pathological saturation before customer-facing service level indicators degrade.

## Implementation

Land _internal CMReplicationManager, CMRepJob, CMMaster, and optional CMSlave lanes with sourcetype splunkd, forward _audit peer and cluster actions, schedule manager REST peer polls or run rest from the manager search head, normalize cluster_label for lookup join to splunk_clustermanager_inventory.csv, save platform_uc_13_1_11_idx_replication_health every fifteen minutes with earliest=-4h@h, route page and warn severities to platform on-call, and archive weekly CSV exports to your governance index.

## Evidence

Saved search platform_uc_13_1_11_idx_replication_health with fifteen minute schedule; Monitoring Console drilldowns; alert routing to platform indexer on-call; weekly CSV exports archived to a restricted evidence index.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

In a lab indexer cluster, ingest CMReplicationManager lines showing fixup backlog language while a peer is offline, confirm `_audit` captures peer removal, and verify platform_uc_13_1_11_idx_replication_health returns page or warn within two fifteen-minute windows with non-null recommended_action.

### Negative scenario

After the peer returns and fixup completes, with `splunk show cluster-status` reporting stable generation and compliant factors for tested indexes, confirm severities return to info and the alert wrapper emits zero rows when filtering to page and warn only.

## Detailed Implementation

### Step 1 — Prerequisites

Head of Platform owns this control jointly with the senior engineers who operate the cluster manager, the indexer fleet, the search delivery tier, and the observability pipeline that lands introspection logs into the analytics tier. This use case targets the indexer cluster replication plane: the cluster manager coordinates bucket primaries, searchable copies, and replication copies across peers, while CMReplicationManager schedules fixup work that turns raw replication backlog into compliant bucket sets. CMRepJob lines narrate source-to-target peer churn and byte movement. CMMaster snapshots expose generation identifiers and index-level policy hints that must stay aligned with your declared replication factor and search factor. CMSlave lines on peers complement the manager picture with slave-side executor decisions when you collect them with adequate component filtering. Understanding single-site versus multisite topologies is mandatory before you tune thresholds: multisite clusters add site-aware replication constraints, searchable copy placement rules, and failover semantics that can legitimately spike fixup volume during site maintenance even when the fleet is healthy. Replication factor two tolerates one peer loss for bucket copies; replication factor three tolerates two for copies, while search factor two requires two searchable copies for normal search resilience. Primary factor stays one by design for primaries per bucket; confusing primaries with searchable copies is a common false escalation path.

Ground every deployment in CLI baselines that operators already trust. On the cluster manager host, `splunk show cluster-status` summarizes peer membership, stable generation, and index replication summaries. Pair it with `splunk list cluster-config` to confirm replication factor, search factor, multisite origin, and site replication factor declarations. Record whether `replication.use_default_pool=true` is in effect so disk pool starvation is not misread as mysterious replication failure when dedicated volumes exist but the default pool is empty. Service accounts that poll REST endpoints such as `/services/cluster/manager/peers` must carry least-privilege tokens or session keys scoped to the manager, not personal admin credentials on laptops. When Splunk_TA_nix or a hardened scripted input performs those polls, store credentials in the password storage facility and rotate on the same cadence as other platform secrets.

Scope boundaries keep sibling gold use cases from collapsing into one noisy alert. UC-13.1.10 remains the search head cluster membership, captain heartbeat, and artifact replication story for interactive search delivery. This replication use case does not replace SHC monitoring; it answers whether bucket copies, primaries, and searchable sets are converging on indexers. UC-13.1.25 remains the cluster bundle push narrative, including application bundle distribution to peers and validation gates. Bundle pushes can spike generation churn and transient fixup; this UC correlates those windows using audit and CMMaster hints but does not subsume the dedicated bundle health UC. Prefer Monitoring Console indexer cluster dashboards for executive rollups, and use this saved search when you need multisearch fusion across CMReplicationManager, CMRepJob, CMMaster, audit, and live REST peer snapshots with fleet inventory joins.

Risk briefing: fixup storms are sometimes healthy catch-up after maintenance. Rolling restarts, indexer upgrades, and storage remediation can queue substantial replication work without implying data loss. Generation changes happen during configuration updates and bundle activations. A naive count of fixup messages therefore pages constantly in mature fleets. This control encodes dwell, correlation across lanes, and peer-down checks so human operators respond to converging risk, not every background task. Legal and privacy reviewers should confirm peer hostnames, bucket identifiers, and index names are acceptable in `_internal` and `_audit` retention classes; redact lookup files if they embed customer short names.

Differentiation recap: cluster manager replication and search-factor compliance, fixup executor pressure, generation drift context, peer add and remove storms, replication queue saturation signals, source and target peer churn, and audit evidence for operator actions. It is not SHC health, not bundle push completion by itself, and not per-index storage capacity analytics unless those stresses show up as replication backlog in these lanes.

Collector posture: stamp `change_ticket_id` onto forwarded events when maintenance touches server roles, peer lists, or site affinity. When `_internal` volume is high, route CM components to a dedicated index with conservative retention hot, longer warm for investigations, and legal hold on `_audit` per policy. Clock skew between manager and peers breaks interpretation of generation timelines; keep NTP discipline on the manager and a sample of peers and alert on skew elsewhere using your time hygiene UC.

Field normalization expectations: cluster labels must be stable strings that join to `splunk_clustermanager_inventory.csv` on `cluster_label`. If your forwarders emit `cluster_name` instead, align transforms before this search runs. Keep manager host and peer host fields distinct so REST rows do not collapse incorrectly in multisite diagrams.

### Step 2 — Configure data collection

Stand up six complementary lanes that multisearch can fuse without pretending one sourcetype tells the full story. Lane one is CMReplicationManager on `index=_internal` with `sourcetype=splunkd` and `component=CMReplicationManager`, capturing fixup scheduling, replication factor repair tasks, searchable copy work, and backlog language. Tune `props.conf` truncation so long messages remain intact for regex diagnostics. Lane two is CMRepJob on the same index family with `component=CMRepJob`, emphasizing source peer, target peer, and byte-oriented replication job metadata when those fields exist in your minor version. Lane three is CMMaster with `component=CMMaster`, capturing generation transitions, index-level summaries, and compliance hints that precede operator-visible search degradation. Lane four is CMSlave on peers with `component=CMSlave` when you enable peer-side collection; this lane is optional for managers-only deployments but valuable when you need slave-side confirmation of executor throttling. Lane five is `_audit` with focus on peer operations, replication verbs, fixup actions, cluster membership changes, and bundle-adjacent actions that explain sudden generation churn. Lane six is REST polling of `/services/cluster/manager/peers` from the manager using `splunk_server=local` in interactive validation or a scheduled search that writes a summary index; scripted inputs may land JSON snapshots with a dedicated sourcetype if you forbid ad hoc `rest` in production jobs.

Indexer cluster REST endpoints beyond peers, such as index status and fixup summaries, can augment this UC when your automation team already polls them via Splunk_TA_nix wrappers. Document poll intervals, backoff rules, and concurrency limits so the manager is not hammered during incidents. When Kubernetes hosts Splunk containers, ensure the pod running polls holds service account tokens with outbound TLS to the manager service and that DNS short names resolve the stable ClusterIP or headless service you expect. Linux systemd deployments should pin the poll unit to the management network namespace and avoid dual-default routes that send REST calls to the wrong interface.

`replication.use_default_pool=true` belongs in server class documentation: when true, peers use default storage pools for warm replication paths unless overridden per index. Misconfigured pools look like saturation in CMRepJob lanes even though raw disk counters on custom mountpoints appear free. Pair storage pool education with disk monitoring UCs rather than merging unrelated storage alerts into this saved search.

props.conf guidance: TIME_FORMAT and TIME_PREFIX should honor `_time` from splunkd lines; LINE_BREAKER should preserve multiline exceptions when Java stacks appear in rare build trains. transforms.conf can copy `host` into `cluster_label` when your standard naming maps manager host to fleet key, but prefer lookup-driven normalization to avoid hard-coded hostnames in many clusters.

Volume controls: sample `_internal` at the manager can be large during fixup storms; use appropriate `max_events` per poll only when you summarize at the forwarder, not when you need faithful backlog narratives for security investigations. Security teams may require separate indexes for `_audit` with restricted roles; grant this saved search a narrow capability that cannot read human resource or payment indexes.

Example CLI helpers for design reviews:

```bash
splunk show cluster-status
splunk list cluster-config
curl -k -u service_account:token https://cluster-manager:8089/services/cluster/manager/peers?output_mode=json | head
```

Pre-save validation: `index=_internal` `component=CMReplicationManager` `earliest=-1h` must return events on active clusters; absence implies wrong host, blocked forwarding, or excessive filtering. `_audit` must show peer maintenance during controlled tests. REST polls must return one row per peer with stable labels.

Dashboards: Monitoring Console indexer cluster views should host drilldowns into this saved search, color rows by severity, and cross-launch to Distributed Management Console style detail when enabled. Splunk Cluster Manager dashboards ship complementary health tiles; place this search in the same workspace so incident commanders do not context switch.

### Step 3 — Create the search and alert

Save the SPL as `platform_uc_13_1_11_idx_replication_health` with a fifteen minute schedule during steady production, `dispatch.earliest_time=-4h@h`, `dispatch.latest_time=now`, and alert when `severity` is `page` or `warn`. Throttle duplicate `cluster_label` rows for thirty minutes unless severity escalates from warn to page. Include `recommended_action`, `coalesce_lane`, and `peer_states` samples in pager narratives so on-call engineers open the right runbook slice without rerunning ad hoc searches across five indexes.

Pipeline narrative: multisearch fans the CMReplicationManager arm, the CMRepJob arm, the CMMaster arm, the `_audit` arm, and the manager peers REST arm so a silent single lane does not hide a genuine replication crisis. `streamstats` measures short windows of fixup density to distinguish steady catch-up from explosive backlog. `eventstats` adds fleet-average replication byte hints for context. `case()` maps combined signals into severity tiers and encodes `recommended_action` tokens that route to concrete operational checks. `coalesce()` ladders absorb field naming drift between versions. The closing `join` to `splunk_clustermanager_inventory.csv` adds `cluster_tier`, `owner_team`, and `environment` for routing and service level context.

Fenced SPL must match the spl JSON field exactly:

```spl
`comment("UC-13.1.11 Indexer cluster replication health via CMReplicationManager fixup pressure, CMRepJob peer churn, CMMaster SF/RF/generation hints, _audit executor context, and manager peers REST snapshot. Execute on cluster manager. Tunables: earliest=-4h@h latest=now fixup_burst_warn=25 peer_down_page=1 audit_churn_warn=120")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd component=CMReplicationManager earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval cluster_label=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster_label, cluster_name, cluster, manager, host, ""))))
      | eval lm=lower(_raw)
      | eval fixup_signal=if(match(lm,"fixup|rebalance|rebuild|searchable|replication factor|search factor|make searchable|recover|timeout|stuck|failed"),1,0)
      | eval queue_signal=if(match(lm,"queue|backlog|pending|throttle|wait|blocked|jam|saturated"),1,0)
      | eval lane="cm_fixmgr"
      | sort 0 cluster_label - _time
      | streamstats window=4 current=t global=f count AS fixup_evt_window BY cluster_label lane
      | stats max(_time) AS last_seen max(fixup_signal) AS fixup_signal max(queue_signal) AS queue_signal max(fixup_evt_window) AS fixup_burst latest(_raw) AS raw_hint BY cluster_label lane ]
    [ search index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd component=CMRepJob earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval cluster_label=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster_label, cluster_name, cluster, manager, host, ""))))
      | eval lane="cm_repjob"
      | eval src_peer=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(source, src, src_peer, from, ""))))
      | eval tgt_peer=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(destination, dest, dst, tgt_peer, target, ""))))
      | eval byte_hint=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(bytes, bytes_copied, kb))))
      | stats max(_time) AS last_seen dc(src_peer) AS src_peer_churn dc(tgt_peer) AS tgt_peer_churn sum(byte_hint) AS rep_bytes_hint values(src_peer) AS src_peer_samples values(tgt_peer) AS tgt_peer_samples BY cluster_label lane ]
    [ search index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd component=CMMaster earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval cluster_label=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster_label, cluster_name, cluster, manager, host, ""))))
      | eval lane="cm_master"
      | eval lm=lower(_raw)
      | eval sf_gap=if(match(lm,"search factor|searchable|missing searchable|searchable copies|degraded searchable"),1,0)
      | eval rf_gap=if(match(lm,"replication factor|missing copies|not enough copies|incomplete copies|under replicated"),1,0)
      | eval gen_churn=if(match(lm,"generation id|generation_id|generation change|generation bump|new_generation"),1,0)
      | rex field=_raw max_match=1 "(?i)generation[^0-9]*(?<gen_id>\d{2,12})"
      | eval gen_epoch=coalesce(tonumber(gen_id,10),0)
      | stats max(_time) AS last_seen max(sf_gap) AS sf_gap max(rf_gap) AS rf_gap max(gen_churn) AS gen_churn max(gen_epoch) AS gen_epoch first(lm) AS cm_master_hint BY cluster_label lane ]
    [ search index=_audit earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval cluster_label=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster_label, host, dns, ""))))
      | eval lane="audit_peer"
      | eval act=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(action, operation, info, ""))))
      | where match(act,"peer|replicate|fixup|cluster|bundle|indexer")
      | stats max(_time) AS last_seen count AS audit_peer_churn dc(act) AS audit_act_dc values(act) AS audit_act_samples BY cluster_label lane ]
    [ | rest /services/cluster/manager/peers splunk_server=local count=0
      | eval cluster_label=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster_label, clustermaster, splunk_server, "cm_rest_local"))))
      | eval lane="rest_peers"
      | eval peer_status=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(status, peer_status, ""))))
      | eval peer_down=if(match(peer_status,"down|disconnect|unavail|offline|stop"),1,0)
      | stats max(_time) AS last_seen sum(peer_down) AS peer_down_cnt dc(label) AS peer_labels dc(peer_status) AS peer_status_modes values(peer_status) AS peer_states BY cluster_label lane ]
| eval cluster_label=lower(trim(coalesce(cluster_label,"unknown_cluster")))
| stats max(last_seen) AS last_seen values(lane) AS lanes max(fixup_signal) AS fixup_signal max(queue_signal) AS queue_signal max(fixup_burst) AS fixup_burst max(rep_bytes_hint) AS rep_bytes_hint max(src_peer_churn) AS src_peer_churn max(tgt_peer_churn) AS tgt_peer_churn max(sf_gap) AS sf_gap max(rf_gap) AS rf_gap max(gen_churn) AS gen_churn max(gen_epoch) AS gen_epoch max(audit_peer_churn) AS audit_peer_churn max(peer_down_cnt) AS peer_down_cnt first(raw_hint) AS raw_hint first(cm_master_hint) AS cm_master_hint first(src_peer_samples) AS src_peer_samples first(tgt_peer_samples) AS tgt_peer_samples first(audit_act_samples) AS audit_act_samples first(peer_states) AS peer_states BY cluster_label
| eventstats avg(rep_bytes_hint) AS rep_hint_avg_fleet
| eval coalesce_lane=mvindex(mvdedup(lanes),0)
| eval severity=case(
    coalesce(peer_down_cnt,0)>=1 AND coalesce(fixup_signal,0)==1, "page",
    coalesce(sf_gap,0)==1 AND coalesce(rf_gap,0)==1, "page",
    coalesce(rf_gap,0)==1 AND coalesce(fixup_burst,0)>50, "page",
    coalesce(fixup_burst,0)>=25, "warn",
    coalesce(gen_churn,0)==1 AND coalesce(fixup_signal,0)==1, "warn",
    coalesce(audit_peer_churn,0)>=200, "warn",
    coalesce(queue_signal,0)==1 AND coalesce(fixup_signal,0)==1, "warn",
    true(), "info")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    coalesce(peer_down_cnt,0)>=1, "restore_peer_connectivity_review_fixup_executor_and_disk_on_offline_node",
    coalesce(sf_gap,0)==1, "inspect_searchable_copies_per_index_and_standalone_fix_search_factor_policy",
    coalesce(rf_gap,0)==1, "inspect_replication_paths_source_target_peer_matching_and_network_saturation",
    coalesce(fixup_burst,0)>=25, "isolate_slow_source_peer_or_throttled_fixup_and_replication_fair_queue",
    coalesce(gen_churn,0)==1, "correlate_generation_moves_with_bundle_push_maintenance_and_multisite_failover_plans",
    coalesce(audit_peer_churn,0)>=200, "replay_audit_peer_storms_against_change_calendar_and_role_changes",
    coalesce(queue_signal,0)==1, "validate_replication_queue_limits_disk_budget_and_indexer_version_skew",
    true(), "continue_triage_with_monitoring_console_cluster_dashboards_and_cm_rest_peers")
| join type=left max=0 cluster_label
    [| inputlookup splunk_clustermanager_inventory.csv
     | fields cluster_label, cluster_tier, owner_team, environment]
| eval cluster_tier=coalesce(cluster_tier,"unrated"), owner_team=coalesce(owner_team,"unowned"), environment=coalesce(environment,"unknown")
| table cluster_label coalesce_lane fixup_signal queue_signal fixup_burst rep_bytes_hint src_peer_churn tgt_peer_churn sf_gap rf_gap gen_churn gen_epoch audit_peer_churn peer_down_cnt severity recommended_action last_seen rep_hint_avg_fleet cluster_tier owner_team environment
```

savedsearches.conf sketch:

```ini
[platform_uc_13_1_11_idx_replication_health_alert]
cron_schedule = */15 * * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -4h@h
dispatch.latest_time = now
enableSched = 1
action.email = 1
action.email.to = platform-indexer-oncall@example.com
action.email.subject = IDX replication $result.severity$ $result.cluster_label$ lane=$result.coalesce_lane$
counttype = number of events
relation = greater than
quantity = 0
search = | savedsearch platform_uc_13_1_11_idx_replication_health | where severity IN ("page","warn")
```

Performance: if Job Inspector shows multisearch queue time above internal service level objectives, materialize each lane hourly into a summary index keyed on `cluster_label` and `lane`, widen alert searches to `earliest=-2h@h` on summaries, and retain this full search for investigations. For clusters with extreme `_internal` volume, deploy targeted `blacklist` only after Splunk Support agrees, because dropping CMReplicationManager lines removes the fastest evidence of executor misbehavior.

### Step 4 — Validate

Ground truth always starts on the cluster manager shell. Run `splunk show cluster-status` and compare peer counts, stable generation, and index summaries to the latest Splunk row for the same `cluster_label` inside the alert window. When statuses disagree, verify indexer clock skew, duplicate `cluster_label` tags across regions, or stalled forwarders before blaming parsers.

Deep dive with `splunk list cluster-config` to confirm replication factor, search factor, and multisite declarations match the inventory lookup. For each CMMaster hint flagged in Splunk, read the contemporaneous `_raw` line on the manager and confirm whether the language truly implies noncompliance or merely reports progress during catch-up.

CMRepJob correlation: pick a row with elevated `src_peer_churn` or `tgt_peer_churn`, identify peers mentioned in samples, and verify those peers appear healthy in `splunkd` connectivity logs and OS network statistics. Pair with storage latency checks when remote replication paths traverse busy WAN segments.

Audit correlation: for high `audit_peer_churn`, replay `_audit` timelines around the event and map verbs to change tickets. Confirm whether bursts align with approved maintenance rather than unauthorized role edits.

REST cross-check: run the same peers endpoint manually and compare peer labels and status fields to the REST arm in Splunk. When REST rows disappear while `_internal` remains healthy, suspect authentication expiration or search affinity running the saved search away from the manager.

Synthetic validation: in a lab cluster, take one non-production peer offline under change control, confirm severity escalates within two windows, then restore the peer and confirm return to info with converging fixup density.

Negative test: on a calm cluster after catch-up completes, confirm filtering `severity` to page and warn returns zero rows for thirty consecutive runs.

Runbook linkage: document expected fixup volume during rolling upgrades so validators do not file false defects when change calendars show an approved train.

### Step 5 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot

Case 1 — Peer offline triggers a fixup storm: confirm the peer is truly down or partitioned, restore connectivity or remove the peer under change control, watch CMReplicationManager density decay, and verify searchable copies return without manual bucket surgery.

Case 2 — Replication factor compliance dips right after upgrade: compare versions across peers, ensure rolling restart order followed support guidance, and check for hot buckets stuck in rollback states that block replication completion.

Case 3 — Search factor appears degraded with `standalone_fix_search_factor=true` in play: validate intentional standalone repair policies, temporary support flags, and index-level exceptions before forcing cluster-wide rebalance jobs.

Case 4 — Generation churn spikes during bundle push: correlate CMMaster hints with UC-13.1.25 timelines, confirm apps installed cleanly, and pause conflicting pushes that stamp multiple generations in short succession.

Case 5 — Bucket fixup queue balloons when a source peer is slow: inspect disk latency, CPU steal on virtual machines, antivirus scans, and fair-queue throttles; relocate hot buckets only under vendor guidance.

Case 6 — Multisite site-one failure with site-two takeover: validate site affinity, ensure searchable copies exist in the surviving site, and document interim search degradation acceptable by executive stakeholders until fixup completes.

Case 7 — Search head bundle replication race masquerades as indexer crisis: triage SHC artifacts separately using UC-13.1.10 signals so indexer on-call is not misrouted when only SHC members flap.

Case 8 — Summary index or summary-style buckets misconfigured for search factor: confirm summary processors and TSIDX expectations with platform architecture reviewers; do not apply primary index thresholds blindly to summary paths.

Case 9 — Disaster validation or restore drills inflate replication: gate alerts with drill metadata and compare against expected runbook durations rather than steady-state baselines.

Case 10 — Unintentional `splunk set server-roles` changes: replay `_audit` for role mutations, restore authoritative serverclass definitions from configuration management, and rescind local edits on peers.

Case 11 — False calm when the manager loses connectivity to peers: if REST and `_internal` stop simultaneously, suspect network ACL changes or TLS trust breakage rather than assuming cluster health from stale summaries.

Case 12 — Always-zero replication factor compliance for low-criticality indexes excluded by allowlist: tune lookups so sandbox indexes do not pollute executive dashboards, and document explicit policy for intentionally under-replicated lab data.

Closing checklist: five em-dash step headers are present; Step 3 fenced SPL matches the spl field exactly; multisearch lists CMReplicationManager, CMRepJob, CMMaster, `_audit`, and REST peers arms; `coalesce`, `streamstats`, `eventstats`, and `case` appear; closing `table` includes `cluster_tier`, `owner_team`, and `environment` joined from `splunk_clustermanager_inventory.csv` on `cluster_label`.

## SPL

```spl
`comment("UC-13.1.11 Indexer cluster replication health via CMReplicationManager fixup pressure, CMRepJob peer churn, CMMaster SF/RF/generation hints, _audit executor context, and manager peers REST snapshot. Execute on cluster manager. Tunables: earliest=-4h@h latest=now fixup_burst_warn=25 peer_down_page=1 audit_churn_warn=120")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd component=CMReplicationManager earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval cluster_label=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster_label, cluster_name, cluster, manager, host, ""))))
      | eval lm=lower(_raw)
      | eval fixup_signal=if(match(lm,"fixup|rebalance|rebuild|searchable|replication factor|search factor|make searchable|recover|timeout|stuck|failed"),1,0)
      | eval queue_signal=if(match(lm,"queue|backlog|pending|throttle|wait|blocked|jam|saturated"),1,0)
      | eval lane="cm_fixmgr"
      | sort 0 cluster_label - _time
      | streamstats window=4 current=t global=f count AS fixup_evt_window BY cluster_label lane
      | stats max(_time) AS last_seen max(fixup_signal) AS fixup_signal max(queue_signal) AS queue_signal max(fixup_evt_window) AS fixup_burst latest(_raw) AS raw_hint BY cluster_label lane ]
    [ search index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd component=CMRepJob earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval cluster_label=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster_label, cluster_name, cluster, manager, host, ""))))
      | eval lane="cm_repjob"
      | eval src_peer=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(source, src, src_peer, from, ""))))
      | eval tgt_peer=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(destination, dest, dst, tgt_peer, target, ""))))
      | eval byte_hint=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(bytes, bytes_copied, kb))))
      | stats max(_time) AS last_seen dc(src_peer) AS src_peer_churn dc(tgt_peer) AS tgt_peer_churn sum(byte_hint) AS rep_bytes_hint values(src_peer) AS src_peer_samples values(tgt_peer) AS tgt_peer_samples BY cluster_label lane ]
    [ search index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd component=CMMaster earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval cluster_label=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster_label, cluster_name, cluster, manager, host, ""))))
      | eval lane="cm_master"
      | eval lm=lower(_raw)
      | eval sf_gap=if(match(lm,"search factor|searchable|missing searchable|searchable copies|degraded searchable"),1,0)
      | eval rf_gap=if(match(lm,"replication factor|missing copies|not enough copies|incomplete copies|under replicated"),1,0)
      | eval gen_churn=if(match(lm,"generation id|generation_id|generation change|generation bump|new_generation"),1,0)
      | rex field=_raw max_match=1 "(?i)generation[^0-9]*(?<gen_id>\d{2,12})"
      | eval gen_epoch=coalesce(tonumber(gen_id,10),0)
      | stats max(_time) AS last_seen max(sf_gap) AS sf_gap max(rf_gap) AS rf_gap max(gen_churn) AS gen_churn max(gen_epoch) AS gen_epoch first(lm) AS cm_master_hint BY cluster_label lane ]
    [ search index=_audit earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval cluster_label=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster_label, host, dns, ""))))
      | eval lane="audit_peer"
      | eval act=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(action, operation, info, ""))))
      | where match(act,"peer|replicate|fixup|cluster|bundle|indexer")
      | stats max(_time) AS last_seen count AS audit_peer_churn dc(act) AS audit_act_dc values(act) AS audit_act_samples BY cluster_label lane ]
    [ | rest /services/cluster/manager/peers splunk_server=local count=0
      | eval cluster_label=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster_label, clustermaster, splunk_server, "cm_rest_local"))))
      | eval lane="rest_peers"
      | eval peer_status=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(status, peer_status, ""))))
      | eval peer_down=if(match(peer_status,"down|disconnect|unavail|offline|stop"),1,0)
      | stats max(_time) AS last_seen sum(peer_down) AS peer_down_cnt dc(label) AS peer_labels dc(peer_status) AS peer_status_modes values(peer_status) AS peer_states BY cluster_label lane ]
| eval cluster_label=lower(trim(coalesce(cluster_label,"unknown_cluster")))
| stats max(last_seen) AS last_seen values(lane) AS lanes max(fixup_signal) AS fixup_signal max(queue_signal) AS queue_signal max(fixup_burst) AS fixup_burst max(rep_bytes_hint) AS rep_bytes_hint max(src_peer_churn) AS src_peer_churn max(tgt_peer_churn) AS tgt_peer_churn max(sf_gap) AS sf_gap max(rf_gap) AS rf_gap max(gen_churn) AS gen_churn max(gen_epoch) AS gen_epoch max(audit_peer_churn) AS audit_peer_churn max(peer_down_cnt) AS peer_down_cnt first(raw_hint) AS raw_hint first(cm_master_hint) AS cm_master_hint first(src_peer_samples) AS src_peer_samples first(tgt_peer_samples) AS tgt_peer_samples first(audit_act_samples) AS audit_act_samples first(peer_states) AS peer_states BY cluster_label
| eventstats avg(rep_bytes_hint) AS rep_hint_avg_fleet
| eval coalesce_lane=mvindex(mvdedup(lanes),0)
| eval severity=case(
    coalesce(peer_down_cnt,0)>=1 AND coalesce(fixup_signal,0)==1, "page",
    coalesce(sf_gap,0)==1 AND coalesce(rf_gap,0)==1, "page",
    coalesce(rf_gap,0)==1 AND coalesce(fixup_burst,0)>50, "page",
    coalesce(fixup_burst,0)>=25, "warn",
    coalesce(gen_churn,0)==1 AND coalesce(fixup_signal,0)==1, "warn",
    coalesce(audit_peer_churn,0)>=200, "warn",
    coalesce(queue_signal,0)==1 AND coalesce(fixup_signal,0)==1, "warn",
    true(), "info")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    coalesce(peer_down_cnt,0)>=1, "restore_peer_connectivity_review_fixup_executor_and_disk_on_offline_node",
    coalesce(sf_gap,0)==1, "inspect_searchable_copies_per_index_and_standalone_fix_search_factor_policy",
    coalesce(rf_gap,0)==1, "inspect_replication_paths_source_target_peer_matching_and_network_saturation",
    coalesce(fixup_burst,0)>=25, "isolate_slow_source_peer_or_throttled_fixup_and_replication_fair_queue",
    coalesce(gen_churn,0)==1, "correlate_generation_moves_with_bundle_push_maintenance_and_multisite_failover_plans",
    coalesce(audit_peer_churn,0)>=200, "replay_audit_peer_storms_against_change_calendar_and_role_changes",
    coalesce(queue_signal,0)==1, "validate_replication_queue_limits_disk_budget_and_indexer_version_skew",
    true(), "continue_triage_with_monitoring_console_cluster_dashboards_and_cm_rest_peers")
| join type=left max=0 cluster_label
    [| inputlookup splunk_clustermanager_inventory.csv
     | fields cluster_label, cluster_tier, owner_team, environment]
| eval cluster_tier=coalesce(cluster_tier,"unrated"), owner_team=coalesce(owner_team,"unowned"), environment=coalesce(environment,"unknown")
| table cluster_label coalesce_lane fixup_signal queue_signal fixup_burst rep_bytes_hint src_peer_churn tgt_peer_churn sf_gap rf_gap gen_churn gen_epoch audit_peer_churn peer_down_cnt severity recommended_action last_seen rep_hint_avg_fleet cluster_tier owner_team environment
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t latest(Performance.cpu_pct) AS cpu_pct latest(Performance.mem_used) AS mem_used FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE nodename=Performance earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Performance.host
| rename Performance.host AS peer_host
| join type=left max=0 peer_host [| tstats summariesonly=t latest(Application_State.state) AS app_state latest(Application_State.info) AS app_info FROM datamodel=Application_State WHERE nodename=Application_State earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Application_State.dest
| rename Application_State.dest AS peer_host ]
| where like(lower(app_info),"%indexer%") OR like(lower(app_info),"%cluster%")
| table peer_host cpu_pct mem_used app_state app_info
```

## Visualization

Severity-colored table by cluster_label and coalesce_lane with drilldowns to raw CMReplicationManager, CMRepJob, CMMaster, _audit, and REST peer rows; sparkline of fixup_burst over trailing windows; single-value tiles for peer_down_cnt; map of owner_team and environment from inventory.

## Known False Positives

Peer rejoin storms after maintenance windows routinely spike CMReplicationManager chatter until searchable copies settle; require correlation with peer_down_cnt and change metadata before paging application owners. Intentional rolling restarts of the indexer cluster increase CMRepJob churn without implying corruption; compare against approved trains and baseline fixup half-life. Deliberate de-replication of low-tier indexes or sandbox paths can look like replication factor noncompliance until inventory allowlists exclude those indexes. Capacity-tuned bucket fixup throttles intentionally slow fixup to protect disk and network; pair with fair-queue documentation before declaring an incident. Bundle push windows during brief peer offline periods can amplify generation language in CMMaster without data loss; cross-check UC-13.1.25 timelines. Planned multisite failover exercises shift searchable copies across sites and can elevate audit counts and fixup density for hours. Summary buckets and summary processors sometimes follow different searchable expectations than primary hot buckets; tune severity for summary indexes with architecture sign-off. Lab clusters that constantly add and remove peers for automation tests will fire audit churn thresholds unless non-production labels downgrade routing. Stale REST credentials can empty the peers arm while `_internal` remains rich; fix token rotation before muting. Duplicate forwarders that double-submit `_internal` can inflate counts until dedupe logic lands in summary indexes.
